newsletter-digest.title: Newsletter Digest
newsletter-digest.description: Daily intelligence gathered from 20+ product and engineering newsletters.

content.title: The Shift to Agentic Orchestration
content.summary:
We are witnessing a fundamental shift in how we interact with LLMs. For the past two years, the industry was obsessed with the 'Chat' interface—a simple request-response cycle. However, the data suggests that the real value lies in 'Agentic Orchestration' where the LLM is the controller of a multi-step workflow.

This transition requires a complete rethink of our technical stack. Traditional vector databases are being augmented with 'Knowledge Graphs' to provide agents with relational context.

content.title: The Death of Horizontal Generative AI
content.summary: The first wave of GenAI was dominated by horizontal tools—writing assistants and image generators. While these were impressive, they are rapidly becoming features of the OS or the browser. The next massive opportunity lies in 'Vertical SaaS' where AI is deeply integrated into a specific industry's reality.

content.title: State of Product-Led Growth
content.summary: Product-led growth is entering a new phase where 'Sales-Assisted PLG' is no longer an edge case but a requirement for moving upmarket. Companies like Notion and Airtable are redefining how high-touch sales and self-serve work together.

content.title: Engineering Systems at Scale
content.summary: Building distributed systems in 2026 is less about infrastructure management and more about 'Logic Observability'. As we offload more logic to autonomous agents, the bottleneck moves to how we verify their decision-making in real-time.

linkedin-drafts.title: LinkedIn Intel
linkedin-drafts.description: Scoping high-signal product management discussions from Reddit and drafting them for LinkedIn.

content.title: The 'Product Engineer' is the new 10x developer
content.summary: Draft focusing on the convergence of engineering and product roles in startups. Engineering muscle + Product intuition = Speed.

youtube-summaries.title: YouTube Intel
youtube-summaries.description: Extracting core insights from 1-hour engineering podcasts into 5-minute actionable summaries.

content.title: How Vercel builds their Design System
content.summary: Key takeaway: Components are built for 'flexibility at the edges' rather than rigid consistency. Shared state management is the bottleneck.
